meta.description: Full UPSC CSE syllabus data structure for MentorsDaily portal — used for AI module generation, syllabus tracker, and standardised module templates.

subtopics.name: Prehistoric India — Stone Age, Paleolithic, Mesolithic, Neolithic
Indus Valley Civilization — Sites, Features, Decline
Vedic Age — Early & Later Vedic, Rig Veda, Upanishads
Mauryan Empire — Chandragupta, Ashoka, Administration, Decline
Gupta Empire — Golden Age, Administration, Culture
South India — Sangam Age, Cholas, Cheras, Pandyas
Art, Architecture & Literature of Ancient India
Early Medieval — Rajputs, Arab Invasion, Tripartite Struggle
Delhi Sultanate — Slave, Khilji, Tughlaq, Sayyid, Lodi Dynasties
Mughal Empire — Babur to Aurangzeb, Administration, Culture
European Arrival — Portuguese, Dutch, British, French
British Expansion — Battles, Subsidiary Alliance, Doctrine of Lapse
Indian National Congress — Formation, Phases, Leaders
Rise of Nationalism — Partition of Bengal, Swadeshi
Gandhian Era — Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience, Quit India
Social Reform Movements — Ram Mohan Roy, Arya Samaj, etc.
Partition, Independence & Integration of Princely States
World War I — Causes, Events, Treaty of Versailles
World War II — Causes, Events, Aftermath
Origin & Evolution of Earth, Solar System
Interior of Earth — Layers, Seismic Waves
Plate Tectonics — Theories, Boundaries, Earthquakes, Volcanoes
Physical Divisions of India — Mountains, Plateaus, Plains, Islands
Indian Rivers — Himalayan & Peninsular, Drainage Patterns
Indian Climate — Monsoon, Seasons, Rainfall Distribution
Soils of India — Types, Distribution, Conservation
Transport & Communication — Roads, Railways, Ports, Airports
Major Rivers, Lakes, Mountains of the World
World Natural Resources — Minerals, Oil, Gas
Regional Geography — Africa, Europe, Americas, SE Asia
Economic Geography — Trade Routes, Ports, SEZs
Indian Map — States, Capitals, Districts, Rivers
Making of Constitution — Constituent Assembly, Committees
Fundamental Rights — Articles 12-35, Exceptions, Case Laws
Key Features — Federal, Secular, Socialist, Democratic
Vice President, Prime Minister, Council of Ministers
Parliament — Rajya Sabha, Lok Sabha, Sessions, Bills
Supreme Court — Jurisdiction, Judicial Review, PILs
Election Commission — Powers, EVMs, Model Code
Emergency Provisions — National, President's Rule, Financial
Constitutional Bodies — UPSC, Finance Commission, NCSC, NCST
Statutory Bodies — CIC, CVC, CBI, NHRC, SEBI, RBI
Executive Bodies — NITI Aayog, NDC, NEC, ISC
RTI Act, Right to Education, Food Security Act
Micro vs Macro Economics — Demand, Supply, Elasticity
National Income — GDP, GNP, NNP, NDP, Per Capita
Inflation — Types, WPI, CPI, Control Measures
Planning in India — Five Year Plans, NITI Aayog
SDGs, Human Development Index, UNDP Reports
Economic Survey Key Points & Budget Analysis
RBI — Functions, Monetary Policy Committee, Tools
Banking System — Commercial, Cooperative, NBFCs, Payments Banks
Financial Inclusion — Jan Dhan, Mudra, SHG-Bank Linkage
NPA, Bad Banks, IBC, SARFAESI Act
Union Budget — Components, Revenue vs Capital
Taxation — Direct (Income Tax, Corporate Tax), Indirect (GST)
Fiscal Deficit, Revenue Deficit, FRBM Act
Balance of Payments — Current, Capital Account
Foreign Trade Policy — Exports, Imports, FTAs
FDI, FII, ECBs — Routes, Limits, Sectors
Exchange Rate — Fixed, Floating, Currency Convertibility
IMF, World Bank, ADB, AIIB — Functions, India
Green Revolution, White Revolution, Blue Revolution
Agricultural Marketing — MSP, APMC, e-NAM, FPOs
Food Security — PDS, NFSA, Buffer Stock
Ecosystem — Components, Types, Functions, Food Chain
Biogeochemical Cycles — Carbon, Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Water
Types of Biodiversity — Genetic, Species, Ecosystem
Threatened Species — IUCN Red List, Schedule I-VI
Protected Areas — National Parks, WLS, Tiger Reserves, BRs
Invasive Species, Extinction Crisis, Sixth Mass Extinction
Project Tiger, Project Elephant, Crocodile Project
Greenhouse Effect, Global Warming, Climate Change
UNFCCC — Kyoto Protocol, Paris Agreement, NDCs
Air Pollution — AQI, Pollutants, Health Effects
Water Pollution — Sources, River Pollution, Treatment
Forest Conservation Act, Forest Rights Act
National Green Tribunal — Powers, Landmark Cases
Physics — Motion, Waves, Light, Electricity, Magnetism
Chemistry — Elements, Reactions, Acids, Bases, Polymers
Human Body — Systems, Diseases, Nutrition, Vitamins
Defence — DRDO, BrahMos, Missiles, INS, HAL
Nuclear Technology — Reactors, Three Stage Programme
International Space Organisations — NASA, ESA, SpaceX trends
Artificial Intelligence, ML, Deep Learning basics
Biotechnology — GMO, CRISPR, Stem Cells, Vaccines
Renewable Energy — Solar, Wind, Hydro, Green Hydrogen
Communicable Diseases — TB, Malaria, Dengue, COVID
National Health Mission, Ayushman Bharat, AIIMS
Medical Ethics, Clinical Trials, Drug Regulation (CDSCO)

subjects.name: Current Affairs & Government Schemes
subjects.note: Ongoing — must be updated monthly throughout preparation

subtopics.name: Parliament Sessions — Bills Passed, Key Debates
Economic Decisions — RBI Policy, Budget, GDP Data
Social Issues — Caste, Gender, Reservation, Education
Multilateral Organisations — G20, SCO, BRICS, Quad
UN System — General Assembly, Security Council, Agencies
Agriculture & Rural Schemes — PM-KISAN, MGNREGA, PMAY-G
Urban & Infrastructure — Smart Cities, AMRUT, PM Gati Shakti
Education Schemes — NEP 2020, Samagra Shiksha, DIKSHA
Health Schemes — Ayushman Bharat, NHM, Poshan Abhiyan
Financial Inclusion — Jan Dhan, MUDRA, Stand-Up India
Women & Child — Beti Bachao, PMMVY, One Stop Centres
National Awards — Bharat Ratna, Padma, Gallantry, Nobel
Global Reports — HDI, Press Freedom, EoDB, WEF Reports
G20, G7, BRICS, SCO, SAARC Summits
Sports — Olympics, CWG, India Medals, New Records
Science & Research — New Discoveries, ISRO Milestones
Classical Dances — Bharatanatyam, Kathak, Odissi, Kuchipudi, etc.
Classical Music — Hindustani vs Carnatic, Instruments, Gharanas
Paintings — Miniature, Ajanta, Mughal, Rajput, Madhubani, Warli
Architecture — Temple Styles (Nagara, Dravidian), Cave Art, Stupas
Fairs, Festivals, Folk Arts of India
Languages — Scheduled Languages, Classical Language Status

csat.name: CSAT — Civil Services Aptitude Test (Paper II)
csat.note: Qualifying paper only. Score not counted in merit.

subtopics.name: Number System — LCM, HCF, Fractions, BODMAS
Percentages, Profit & Loss, Simple & Compound Interest
Data Interpretation — Tables, Bar, Line, Pie Charts

gs_paper_1.name: GS Paper I — Indian Heritage, Culture, History, Society & Geography

topics.name: Indus Valley to Modern — Cultural Continuity
Salient aspects of Art Forms — Cave Paintings to Miniatures
Literature — Sanskrit, Regional, Modern Indian Literature
Contributions of Indian Diaspora to World Culture
18th Century India — Decline of Mughal, Rise of Regional Powers
Economic Impact of British Rule — Drain Theory, Deindustrialisation
Freedom Struggle — Social Reforms, Mass Movements
World History — Colonialism, Decolonisation, Cold War, Reunification
Salient Features of Indian Society — Diversity, Unity
Role of Women — Status, Empowerment, Issues
Population & Urbanisation — Migration, Slums, Smart Cities
Social Empowerment — ST, SC, OBC, Minorities
Salient Features of World's Physical Geography
Important Geophysical Phenomena — Earthquakes, Cyclones, Floods

gs_paper_2.name: GS Paper II — Polity, Governance, Constitution, Social Justice & IR

topics.name: Union & State Legislature — Powers, Functioning, Reforms
Executive — President, PM, Cabinet, Governor, CM
Centre-State Relations — Federalism Issues, Cooperative Federalism
Electoral System — Reforms, Electoral Bonds, Campaign Finance
Comparison of Indian Constitution with USA, UK, France, Canada, Australia, South Africa — Key Differences in Federal Structure, Rights, Judiciary, Executive
Separation of Powers & Checks and Balances — Indian Model vs World Models
Dispute Redressal Mechanisms — Tribunals, Lokpal, Ombudsman (India vs World)
Issues in Governance — Corruption, Transparency, Accountability
Vulnerable Sections — SC, ST, Women, Children, Elderly, Disabled
India's Foreign Policy — Principles, Evolution, Recent Shifts
India & Neighbourhood — Pakistan, China, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Bangladesh
India & Major Powers — USA, Russia, EU, Japan
UN & International Bodies — Reforms, India's Role
Multilateral Groupings — SCO, BRICS, G20, Quad, ASEAN
Diaspora Policy, Soft Power, Cultural Diplomacy
Effect of Foreign Policies on India's Interests

gs_paper_3.name: GS Paper III — Economy, Agriculture, Environment, S&T, Security

topics.name: Agriculture — Marketing, Reforms, Subsidies, Food Security
Infrastructure — Energy, Roads, Railways, Ports, Airports
Investment Models — PPP, GIFT City, Industrial Corridors
Indian Economy & Globalisation — WTO, Trade Policy
Science & Tech in Economy — Digital Economy, Startups
Science & Tech Developments in India — Achievements
Awareness of IT, Space, Biotech, Nano, IP
New Technologies — AI, ML, IoT, Blockchain in Governance
Defence Technology — Make in India, DRDO, HAL
Climate Change — Paris Agreement, NDCs, Carbon Markets
Disaster Management — Types, NDMA, Early Warning Systems
Linkages between Development & Spread of Extremism
Internal Security & Role of External State Actors
Organised Crime — Nexus with Terrorism, Drug Trade
Cyber Security — Threats, CERT-In, National Cyber Policy
Security Forces — Structure, CAPF, Intelligence Agencies

gs_paper_4.name: GS Paper IV — Ethics, Integrity & Aptitude

topics.name: Human Values — Lessons from Lives of Great Leaders
Role of Family, Society, Educational Institutions in Values
Attitude — Content, Structure, Function, Influence on Behaviour
Aptitude & Foundational Values for Civil Services
Contributions of Moral Thinkers — India & World
Public Service Values — Integrity, Impartiality, Non-Partisanship
Probity in Governance — RTI, Code of Conduct, Citizen's Charter
Corporate Governance, Whistle Blowing, Ethical Trade
Conflict of Interest, Abuse of Power, Accountability
Case Studies Analysis — Practical Problem Solving

subjects.name: Case Studies Practice
subjects.note: 10 marks per case study in exam. Practice minimum 50 case studies.

topics.name: Personal Ethical Conflicts — Family vs Duty
Essay Writing — Structure, Introduction, Body, Conclusion
Section A Themes — Philosophic, Abstract, Life, Values
Section B Themes — Social, Economic, Political, S&T, Environment

optionals.note: Student selects ONE optional (2 papers × 250 marks = 500 marks total). Top 10 optionals listed.

topics.name: Post Mauryan to Gupta — Economy, Society, Culture
Advent of Islam — Arab & Turkish Invasions
Mughal Empire — Detailed Political & Cultural History

papers.name: Paper II — Modern World & Modern India

topics.name: World War I & II — Deep Analysis
British India — Expansion, Administration, Social Policy
Indian Freedom Struggle — Phases, Movements, Leaders
Independent India — Constitution, Nation Building, Integration
Political Geography — Nation State, Boundaries, Geopolitics
Physical Setting — Relief, Drainage, Climate, Soils
Natural Resources — Land, Water, Minerals, Energy
Agriculture — Land Use, Cropping Patterns, Irrigation
Transport & Trade — Networks, Impact on Development
Regional Development — Planning Regions, Backward Area
Introduction — Meaning, Scope, Significance, New PA
Principles of Organisation — Hierarchy, Span, Unity
Decision Making — Simon's Model, Bounded Rationality
Evolution of Indian Administration — British to Present
Central Administration — Cabinet Secretariat, PMO, Ministries
State Administration — Governor, CM, Secretariat, DM
Civil Services — Recruitment, Training, UPSC, Service Rules
Development Programmes — NREGA, IRDP, Rural Development
e-Governance & New Public Management in India
Sociology — Discipline, Nature, Relationship with other Sciences
Sociology as Science — Scientific Method, Objectivity
Research Methods — Survey, Interview, Observation, Case Study
Sociological Thinkers — Marx, Durkheim, Weber, Parsons, Merton
Stratification & Mobility — Class, Caste, Race, Gender
Works & Economic Life — Division of Labour, Capitalism
Systems of Kinship — Types, Descent, Marriage
Perspectives on Indian Society — Indology, Structural, Marxist
Caste System — Features, Changes, Dominant Caste, Dalit Movement
Tribal Communities — Issues, Forest Rights, Development
Social Classes in India — Agrarian & Industrial
Religion & Society in India — Communalism, Fundamentalism
Social Change in Modern India — Colonialism, Nationalism
Women in India — Patriarchy, Violence, Feminist Movement
Vision of Society — Gandhi, Ambedkar, Tagore, Nehru

papers.name: Paper I — Political Theory & Indian Politics

topics.name: Concepts — State, Sovereignty, Power, Authority, Legitimacy
Political Ideologies — Liberalism, Marxism, Socialism, Fascism
Indian Constitutional Framework — DR Ambedkar, Nehru
Indian Party System — Evolution, Coalitions, Regional Parties
Federalism in India — Cooperative, Competitive, Issues
Pressure Groups, Social Movements, Civil Society

papers.name: Paper II — Comparative Politics & International Relations

topics.name: State in Comparative Perspective — Democracy, Authoritarian
Approaches to International Relations — Realism, Liberalism, Marxism
Cold War & Post-Cold War World Order
UN System — Organs, Agencies, Reforms, Effectiveness
India & Major Powers — USA, Russia, China, EU
India & South Asia, SAARC, Neighbourhood Policy
New Issues — Terrorism, Climate, Cyber, Human Security
Micro Economics — Demand, Supply, Market Structures
Macro Economics — National Income, IS-LM Model
Open Economy Macroeconomics — BOP, Exchange Rate
Statistics for Economics — Regression, Sampling, Testing
Growth Models — Harrod-Domar, Solow, Endogenous Growth
Indian Economy — Structure, Features, Colonial Legacy
Planning & Development Strategy — Five Year Plans, NITI
Industry & Services — Policy, SEZ, FDI, Start-ups
Money, Banking & Inflation — RBI Policy, Tools
Public Finance — Budget, Taxation, GST, Fiscal Deficit
External Sector — Trade, BOP, FDI, WTO Issues
Economic Reforms since 1991 — LPG, Outcomes, Debates
Social Sector — Education, Health, HDI, SDG Progress

anthropology.name: Anthropology Optional
anthropology.note: Most scoring optional. High success rate for science graduates.

topics.name: Prehistoric Cultures — Palaeolithic to Iron Age
Social Anthropology — Family, Marriage, Kinship, Religion
Major Theoretical Perspectives — Evolutionism to Post-Modern
Caste System — Features, Critique, Jajmani System
Social Change — Five Year Plans, Land Reform Impact
Ethnographic Profiles — Major Tribes (Gond, Bhil, etc.)
Biological Anthropology — Genetics, Blood Groups, Forensic

papers.name: Paper I — History & Problems of Philosophy

topics.name: Indian Philosophy — Nyaya, Vaisheshika, Sankhya, Yoga
Indian Philosophy — Mimamsa, Vedanta, Buddhism, Jainism
Epistemology — Sources of Knowledge, Perception, Inference

papers.name: Paper II — Applied Ethics & Contemporary Issues

topics.name: Applied Ethics — Bioethics, Environmental Ethics, Business
Philosophy of Religion — God, Proofs, Evil, Liberation
Social & Political Philosophy — Justice, Rights, Democracy
Analytical Philosophy — Russell, Wittgenstein, Logical Positivism
Philosophy of Mind — Mind-Body Problem, Consciousness, AI
Contemporary Indian Thought — Vivekananda, Aurobindo, Gandhi, Ambedkar
Biological Basis of Behaviour — Brain, NS, Sensation
Abnormal Psychology — Anxiety, Mood, Psychotic Disorders
Social Psychology — Group Dynamics, Conformity, Obedience
Health Psychology — Stress, Coping, Lifestyle Disorders
Educational Psychology — Learning Disabilities, Inclusive Ed
Application to Social Problems — Poverty, Crime, Violence

module_templates.description: Standard module template for all students. Pace multiplier adjusts days. Content fixed.

fast_track.suitable_for: Students with prior UPSC knowledge or strong academic background

standard_track.suitable_for: First-time serious aspirants with good study habits

extended_track.suitable_for: Working professionals or students with limited daily study time

test_standardisation.note: Same question paper for all students on same module. Enables cross-student comparison.

ai_module_generator_schema.description: Schema AI reads to generate custom module plan for student

subjects_priority: array of subject IDs — high priority first
already_covered_topics: array of subtopic IDs student marks as done
weak_topics: array of subtopic IDs from past test data
input_fields.optional_subject: opt_history | opt_geography | opt_pub_admin | opt_sociology | opt_polsci | opt_economics | opt_anthropology | opt_philosophy | opt_psychology

warnings: array of warnings if target date too tight
